name: cors-allowlist-enforcement
question: Does main.rs still build the CORS layer from FEEDBACKMONK_CORS_ORIGINS and apply it to BOTH the submission and attachments routers, and does public_cors_layer keep the credentialed echo-origin policy (allow_credentials + AllowOrigin::list, never a wildcard)? (With --full: does the cors_preflight integration test pass?)

output.schema: PASS or FAIL <n> with probe-level offender detail. Exit 0 on PASS, 1 on FAIL, 2 on environment failure.

consultation.typical_sessions_using: every-commit touching feedbackmonk-api routing or the CORS layer

consumer_scope: feedbackmonk-api: every commit touching crates/feedbackmonk-api/src/main.rs (router composition) or crates/feedbackmonk-api/src/cors.rs (CORS policy).
fallback: Probe A: read crates/feedbackmonk-api/src/main.rs and confirm build_app calls public_cors_layer(...), reads FEEDBACKMONK_CORS_ORIGINS, and applies .layer(<cors>) to both submission_router(...) and attachments_router(...). Probe B: read crates/feedbackmonk-api/src/cors.rs and confirm public_cors_layer uses .allow_credentials(true) + AllowOrigin::list(...) and no wildcard (AllowOrigin::any / allow_origin(Any) / cors::Any). Probe C: cargo test -p feedbackmonk-api --test cors_preflight.

provenance.authored_by: post-DEC-FBR-IMPL-09 follow-up (CORS fix for GitCellar customer #1)
provenance.rationale: DEC-FBR-IMPL-09 added a credentialed CORS layer to the public widget endpoints to fix the GitCellar embed blocker (preflight OPTIONS -> 405). tests/cors_preflight.rs exercises public_cors_layer in isolation, so it cannot catch a wiring-removal regression (deleting .layer(cors) from build_app) — the exact way the bug would silently return. This oracle reads the wiring + policy from source to close that gap. Realizes the code-state guard half of DEC-FBR-04's domain-allowlist-for-widget-embed.
